title: Map-Reduce over Documents
constraints: The map phase must process documents in parallel, not one at a time
Each document must be summarized individually before the reduce step
The reduce step must aggregate all summaries into a single coherent report
The solution must handle at least 20 documents
hints: In LangGraph, use the Send API to dynamically fan out to one node per document, then collect results in a reduce node
Keep individual summaries short (2-3 sentences) so the reduce step has manageable input
The reduce step should synthesize themes and patterns across summaries, not just concatenate them

starterCode.langgraph:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, SystemMessage
import time

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# Simulated document corpus
documents = [f"Document {i}: This is a report about topic {chr(65 + i % 26)} covering various aspects of the subject including analysis, findings, and recommendations for Q{(i % 4) + 1} 2024." for i in range(20)]

# BUG: Documents are processed sequentially — extremely slow for 20+ docs
# TODO: Implement map-reduce pattern with parallel document processing
def process_documents(docs: list[str]) -> str:
    start = time.time()
    summaries = []

    for doc in docs:
        summary = llm.invoke([
            SystemMessage(content="Summarize this document in 2-3 sentences."),
            HumanMessage(content=doc),
        ])
        summaries.append(summary.content)

    # Combine all summaries into final report
    combined = "\n".join(summaries)
    report = llm.invoke([
        SystemMessage(content="Synthesize these document summaries into a cohesive executive report. Identify key themes and patterns."),
        HumanMessage(content=combined),
    ])

    elapsed = time.time() - start
    return f"[Processed {len(docs)} docs in {elapsed:.2f}s]\n{report.content}"

result = process_documents(documents)
print(result)
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
import time

# Simulated document corpus
documents = [f"Document {i}: This is a report about topic {chr(65 + i % 26)} covering various aspects of the subject including analysis, findings, and recommendations for Q{(i % 4) + 1} 2024." for i in range(20)]

analyst = Agent(
    role="Document Analyst",
    goal="Summarize and synthesize documents",
    backstory="You are a senior research analyst who distills large document sets into actionable reports.",
)

# BUG: Documents are processed sequentially — extremely slow for 20+ docs
# TODO: Implement map-reduce pattern with parallel document processing
def process_documents(docs: list[str]) -> str:
    start = time.time()
    tasks = []

    for doc in docs:
        task = Task(
            description=f"Summarize this document in 2-3 sentences: '{doc}'",
            expected_output="A 2-3 sentence summary",
            agent=analyst,
        )
        tasks.append(task)

    tasks.append(Task(
        description="Synthesize all the document summaries above into a cohesive executive report. Identify key themes and patterns.",
        expected_output="An executive summary report",
        agent=analyst,
    ))

    crew = Crew(agents=[analyst], tasks=tasks)
    result = crew.kickoff()

    elapsed = time.time() - start
    return f"[Processed {len(docs)} docs in {elapsed:.2f}s]\n{result}"

result = process_documents(documents)
print(result)
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner
import time

documents = [f"Document {i}: This is a report about topic {chr(65 + i % 26)} covering various aspects of the subject including analysis, findings, and recommendations for Q{(i % 4) + 1} 2024." for i in range(20)]

summarizer = Agent(
    name="Document Summarizer",
    instructions="Summarize this document in 2-3 sentences.",
)

# BUG: Documents are processed sequentially — extremely slow for 20+ docs
# TODO: Implement map-reduce pattern with parallel document processing
def process_documents(docs: list[str]) -> str:
    start = time.time()
    summaries = []

    for doc in docs:
        result = Runner.run_sync(summarizer, doc)
        summaries.append(result.final_output)

    combined = "\n".join(summaries)
    synthesizer = Agent(
        name="Report Synthesizer",
        instructions="Synthesize these document summaries into a cohesive executive report. Identify key themes and patterns.",
    )
    report = Runner.run_sync(synthesizer, combined)

    elapsed = time.time() - start
    return f"[Processed {len(docs)} docs in {elapsed:.2f}s]\n{report.final_output}"

result = process_documents(documents)
print(result)

evaluationCriteria.label: Parallel map phase
evaluationCriteria.description: Documents are summarized in parallel, not sequentially

evaluationCriteria.label: Individual summaries
evaluationCriteria.description: Each document receives its own summary before aggregation

evaluationCriteria.label: Coherent reduce output
evaluationCriteria.description: The final report synthesizes themes across summaries rather than concatenating them

evaluationCriteria.label: Handles 20 documents
evaluationCriteria.description: The pipeline successfully processes at least 20 documents
